name: Northern Mariana Islands (Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands / CNMI)
summary: Northern Mariana Islands (CNMI) is a Pacific Island common-law US Commonwealth — structurally distinctive globally as the only US Commonwealth Pacific Island state established via 1976 Covenant to Establish a Commonwealth (effective 1986), uniquely transitioning from UN Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands to Commonwealth status with substantial customary-land protection. Family-law framework operates under the CNMI Code Title 8 (Domestic Relations) drawing on US-derivative common-law substantive heritage. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by CNMI Code Title 8 Chapter 1. The Supreme Court of the Northern Mariana Islands is the apex domestic court for civil and criminal matters; appellate jurisdiction in certain federal questions lies with the US Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Superior Court (Family Court Division). Customary-land jurisdiction is constitutionally protected via Article XII of CNMI Constitution. Psychology profession is regulated through the CNMI Department of Public Health framework. CNMI is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the welfare-of-the-child principle. CNMI is a Hague Convention 1980 party via US territorial extension effective 1 July 1988.

statutory_framework.title: CNMI Code Title 8 — Domestic Relations
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Domestic Relations Code codifying marriage, divorce, parental responsibility, and child custody. Chapter 1 governs parental responsibility and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Covenant to Establish a Commonwealth
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Covenant establishing CNMI Commonwealth status (effective 1986) with substantial customary-land protection.

statutory_framework.citation: CNMI Constitution Article XII — customary-land protection
statutory_framework.title: CNMI Constitution Article XII
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitutional Article protecting customary-land ownership by persons of Northern Marianas descent.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of the Northern Mariana Islands
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: US Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit + US Supreme Court
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for certain federal questions from CNMI.

professional_regulators.name: CNMI Department of Public Health
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: CNMI family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Covenant to Establish a Commonwealth
key_developments.description: Federal Covenant establishing CNMI Commonwealth status (effective 1986) with substantial customary-land protection.

key_developments.title: CNMI Constitution + Article XII customary-land protection
key_developments.description: Constitution adopted with Article XII protecting customary-land ownership by persons of Northern Marianas descent.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension by US to CNMI effective 1 July 1988.

structural_findings: CNMI operates a US-derivative common-law framework with US Commonwealth status — structurally distinctive globally as only US Commonwealth Pacific Island state established via 1976 Covenant.

Article XII constitutional protection of customary-land ownership by persons of Northern Marianas descent is structurally distinctive globally — only state with this specific constitutional customary-land protection.
Transition from UN Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands to Commonwealth (1976 Covenant) is structurally distinctive — only UN Trust Territory Commonwealth-transition in the corpus.
Hague Convention 1980 applicability via US territorial extension reflects Commonwealth Hague jurisdiction status.
editorial_notes: CNMI jurisdiction sidecar — common-law Pacific US Commonwealth (CNMI Code Title 8 + Covenant 1976 + Constitution Article XII customary-land protection + Hague via US territorial extension 1988). Only US Commonwealth Pacific Island state with UN Trust Territory transition.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Pacific Island + common-law + US-Commonwealth-distinctive + UN-Trust-Territory-transition + Article-XII-customary-land-protection-distinctive + Hague-via-US-territorial-extension clusters within the corpus.
